name: Downtown Austin
description: High-rise downtown core with state government, offices, entertainment districts and Lady Bird Lake waterfront.

name: Clarksville
description: One of Austin's earliest freedmen's communities from 1871, now a small-scale historic district preserving Black cultural history west of downtown.

name: Old West Austin
description: Plateau of early automobile-era suburbs (Old Enfield, Pemberton Heights, Bryker Woods) with historic homes between MoPac and Lamar.

name: Hyde Park
description: Austin's first streetcar suburb from the 1890s, now a dense mix of historic bungalows, apartments and small businesses north of UT.

name: Travis Heights
description: Hilly South Austin neighborhood of 1920s–40s homes and greenbelts just east of South Congress and above Lady Bird Lake.

name: Bouldin Creek
description: Early 20th-century South Austin district mixing historic cottages, diverse residents, murals and creative shops along South 1st and South Congress.

name: Zilker
description: South-central neighborhood next to Zilker Park and Barton Springs, combining 1920s bungalows with newer homes and a strong outdoor and nightlife scene.

name: Tarrytown
description: Leafy, upscale enclave west of MoPac with historic and midcentury homes, parks and Lake Austin access close to downtown.

name: Aldridge Place
description: Early 20th-century subdivision north of UT centered on Hemphill Park, with protected historic homes and landscaped medians.

name: East Cesar Chavez
description: Inner East Austin neighborhood along Cesar Chavez Street with Latino heritage, small bungalows and rapid mixed-use infill near downtown.

name: Holly
description: Formerly industrial strip along Lady Bird Lake centered on the decommissioned Holly Power Plant, now dense housing and new parkland.

name: Montopolis
description: Longstanding East Austin community with roots in an independent town, now a key focus of affordability and displacement debates.

name: Rosewood
description: East Austin neighborhood associated with African American history around Rosewood courts, parks and community institutions.

name: Chestnut
description: Compact East Austin district just east of I-35 with early 20th-century housing stock and an adopted neighborhood plan.

name: Hancock
description: Central neighborhood north of UT containing Hancock Golf Course and a mix of older homes and apartments along Red River and I-35.

name: Allandale
description: North-central neighborhood known for mid-century ranch houses, mature trees, parks and access to Shoal Creek and Burnet Road retail.

name: Rosedale
description: Central Austin area of 1930s–50s cottages near Ramsey Park, medical centers and the Burnet Road commercial corridor.

name: Brentwood
description: Postwar north-central area of small bungalows and duplexes with strong neighborhood identity and walkable Burnet/Koenig corridors.

name: Crestview
description: North-central neighborhood of 1950s ranch homes and transit-oriented infill near Lamar, Burnet and the Crestview commuter rail station.

name: North Loop
description: Compact central neighborhood of older bungalows and eclectic vintage shops, bars and cafes clustered on North Loop Boulevard.

name: Windsor Park
description: East Austin neighborhood of 1950s–60s ranch homes, diverse residents and quick access to Mueller, I-35 and US 290.

name: Cherrywood
description: Tree-lined inner East Austin area of older homes and bungalows between Manor Road and Airport Boulevard, popular with students and creatives.

name: Barton Hills
description: Hilly south-central neighborhood backing onto the Barton Creek Greenbelt, with 1960s homes and strong trail and park access.

name: South Lamar
description: Urbanizing neighborhood centered on South Lamar Boulevard, mixing older single-family streets with dense apartments, bars and restaurants.

name: Riverside
description: Neighborhood along East Riverside Drive with lakeside apartments, student housing and large-scale mixed-use redevelopment plans.

name: Mueller
description: 711-acre redevelopment of the former municipal airport into a dense, walkable, mixed-income urban village with housing, offices, retail and parks.

name: The Domain
description: 303-acre high-density mixed-use district in north Austin often called a second downtown, combining tech offices, apartments and upscale retail.

name: Circle C Ranch
description: 1980s master-planned community in southwest Austin with thousands of homes, parks, an 18-hole golf course and extensive greenbelt access.

name: Avery Ranch
description: Far-northwest master-planned community of over 4,000 homes organized around a golf course, community centers, trails and Hill Country views.

name: Easton Park
description: Large southeast Austin planned unit development with thousands of planned homes, extensive parks, trails and community amenities near McKinney Falls.

name: Whisper Valley
description: Net-zero capable planned community in far east Austin/Manor area built around shared geothermal and solar infrastructure and sustainable design.

name: Onion Creek
description: Established private golf-course community at Austin's southern edge built around Onion Creek Club, with single-family homes, parks and trails.
